title: rubus × retentus
intro: Rubus × retentus, also known as the hybrid black raspberry or black raspberry, is a type of bramble or thorny shrub that belongs to the Rubus genus, which includes other species of raspberries and blackberries. The medicinal properties of Rubus × retentus include its rich content of antioxidants, anthocyanins, and ellagic acid, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties. The health benefits of consuming Rubus × retentus include its potential to reduce the risk of certain cancers, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system, as well as its ability to act as an anti-diabetic agent. Rubus × retentus can be prepared as a herbal remedy through various methods, including tea, tincture, and jam, with the fresh or dried fruit being commonly used in traditional medicine and culinary applications.
